LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANN ENEA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ANN ENEA'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 435.5, WHICH REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE." THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT ENEA'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JUNE 4, 2021. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2021 Set For Trial On May-02-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ANN ENEA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to strike portions of the complaint dealing with punitive damages is denied. Plaintiff pleads: "Defendant showed reckless disregard for the rights of Plaintiff when Defendant intentionally honked a long honk at Plaintiff in his vehicle and clipped the front left quarter panel of Plaintiff's Subaru, causing vehicle damage above the wheel well and injury to Plaintiff. Defendant did not stop her vehicle and fled the scene. Defendant fled the scene of an injury collision and thereby committed a felony. (CVC 20001)."
These are sufficient factual allegations to support a claim for punitive damages at the pleading stage. (See, e.g., Civil Code 3294(c)(1).)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Continued To Aug-18-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ANN ENEA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Ann Enea's motion to strike is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 435.5, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone." The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant Enea's response to the complaint is now due by June 4, 2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
